notes: C3:1817 is not a normal owner start: it is just BRA $17EF, making it a tiny landing pad that redirects execution back into the span frozen in pass 176.
The observed long jump at DF:0E55 still supports treating 1817 itself as executable bytes, but it does not by itself rescue the surrounding 1819..187F region into a clean routine body.

C3:1880 is a real isolated executable splinter: the byte is RTL and there is a same-bank JMP $1880 at C3:2936, so this is best treated as a one-byte return stub rather than left buried in mixed content.

The bytes from 1819..187F remain heavily mixed and include additional raw call-pattern hits that do not stabilize into trustworthy entry owners under cautious inspection, so they are frozen as inline mixed content for now.
Next forward work should resume at 1881 and test whether one of the later same-bank targets in the 1880s or 1890s resolves into a real owner rather than another false-positive call pattern.
